feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
feed.subtitle: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: ACR for Consumption Tax Reporting in Japan
entry.content:
Hi Community,

The Client that I work for wants us to implement ACR for Consumption Tax Reporting in Japan but is not willing to give us access to S-ID. Without the S-ID I am unsure how to proceed with the implementation since I cannot access any notes or KBAs. But they want us to give them the note number so that they would check and give us whatever is necessary(I do not know how this would work)

Since I am new to this kind of implementation, could someone please suggest where I can refer to as an alternative? Is there any other way that I can convince them to give me an S-ID (I want to explain them there are no security issues)?

entry.title: Italy eDocument for zero value invoices
entry.content:
Hi, 

There is a requirement from our Italy clients to develop sales and invoice document types for selling samples to customers. When I made the changes in Development system for a zero value samples invoice, the eDocument is failing. When I created the same invoice with a 0.01 EUR value, the eDocument was transmitted. Does this mean that Italy eDocument does not work for zero value invoices? Could someone please provide a note or KBA to confirm or resolve this?

Thanks,
Vishnu

entry.title: Document Reporting & compliance general questions
entry.content:
Hi DRC Experts, 

I am an FI Consultant who wants to check if DRC is applicable for one of our clients (SAP S4/Hana on Premise). 

I see that SAP uses Peppol Solution heavily in their DRC supported Scenarios. 

Assuming the suppliers dont have Peppol access point, how can DRC Process following scenarios:

  1. Incoming supplier invoices (Paper & PDF) 
  2. Incoming Purchases orders (PDF or XML) 

then I have some specific questions which I couldnt find explicitly in the SAP Documentation:

  1. how does DRC chooses the communication method (email or Peppol) for sending E-invoices?
  2. how does DRC chooses the E-invoice format (ZUGFERD , X-Rechnung etc.)?
  3. Is it possible to send same E-Invoice to multiple customers? how? 

These are critical questions to anwer before we can suggest DRC Solution to our client. 

Thanks for your expertise. 

Regards

Rashed

entry.title: Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition - Binding Creation fails endlessly
entry.content:
Hi, while creating the new instances bulding test instance for Peppol services was done flawless, binding creation was smooth and done fast. We are using the same certificate of ERP in every cloud solution, different type of systems , CPI etc., no problems at all. I couldn't create the binding for the Document and Reporting Compliance production instance, I did it several times and it runs enless until it stops with no positive result. Any suggestions? 

Test

PRD

Thank you very much!

Regards

Uwe

entry.title: Report layout not being used in batch email
entry.content:
Hello all,  we have create a Z report as a copy of S_ALR_87012182.  This is because we needed to add fields and the ability to email. 

I created a layout and set it as the default.  I then created a variant with that layout and assigned it in the batch job.  

When we use the email option either upfront or in batch, the report is received but is not the layout in the variant.  In addition, the report in the email or in download had the field names as column headers rather than the description.  IE the column header says KUNNR instead of customer number.  

Is there something we're missing to get this report to email in the layout we want?  Is it maybe the way the ALV is set up?

Thank you

Cheryl Tackett

entry.content:

entry.title: EORI number into eDocument
entry.content:
Dear All,

we are working for italian customer and it needs to fill the EORI number into xml tag <CodEORI> 1.2.1.3.5.

Please, could you suggest me how to insert this code for each comapny code?

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP Document and Reporting Compliance 

Thanks and regards

entry.title: SAP DRC with SAP CCM Settlements
entry.content:
Hi, we are currently in the ERP to S4HANA transition and already using SAP DRC Document and Reporting Compliance in different countries.
With S4 we will be using SAP CCM Condition Contract Management as a replacement for the old VBO3 rebate agreements. Now we have found out that with CCM the settlements are posting directly to FI/Finance. Therefore we need to have the possibility to have the DRC supported for the settlements for all countries.

According to our analysis, several but not all countries are supported to post settlement documents with DRC - customizable with table EDOTMFKV.

What is your experience with this issue. Is it safe to use CCM settlements for all DRC countries or should we look for another settlement procedure?

Any feedback is welcome.
BR, Günter

entry.title: edoc: Status of the eDocument does not allow the execution of process step
entry.content:
Hello, 

I get an error in the EDOC_COCKPIT: Error triggering message, check interface log for details

In Transaktion SLG1

 

Status of the eDocument does not allow the execution of process step

Message no. EDOCUMENT080

Diagnosis

You are trying to execute the process step .

System Response

The status of the eDocument does not allow the execution of this process step.

Procedure

If you are using the eDocument Cockpit, refer to the process documentation (Help button) for more information on the allowed actions.
If you are using the Create and Submit eDocument report, refer to the EDOPROCCHKFLAGV view for the allowed statuses for each process steps.

I check the EDOPROCCHKFLAGV and see entries for EUINV.

 

What can I do?

Greetings

Marco

entry.title: SAF-T for Romania, adding additional fields in Analytics reporting
entry.content:
Hello experts!

I'm currently implementing the NON_RESIDENT SAF-T report for Romania, in DRC, and one of the current pain point for my user is the lack of some specific fields in the Analytics reports to review the generated SAFT xml file. As of now, we currently have 2 reports tied to it : PURCHASE_INVOICE_LINE_ITEM_A and  SALES_INVOICE_LINE_ITEM_A. And for in the sales line item report, for instance, we dont have the billing document number as an option (in BSEG I have the information under Ref.document field (AWREF)).

After some research, I'm suspecting that my only alternative would be to extend the report to a custom Z, create a new CDS view(s) (copy of C_RO_SAFTSALESINVOICEITEMQ) by adding this and the other fields she need, and then creating a new query to be added to the list of analytical options in that screen.

Wanted to check with the experts if this is the best approach for this and if you guys had any similar experience...

 

Thank you,

 

Fabio Nii

entry.title: IDEA integration for Turkey Incoming invoices
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are currently having the scenario where domestic supplier invoices for Turkey are received from the IDEA (tax authority) portal and are manually booked into the legacy system.

As we transition to S4, we are looking to explore options for direct Ap invoice booking from the tax authority portal in SAP to eliminate the manual process. According to SAP DRC documentation, integration can be achieved via a service provider. Could anyone share their experiences with the service providers they have used for integrating with SAP DRC?

Additionally, does SAP DRC support automatic invoice posting for such received AP invoices. Thank you in advance for your assistance.

entry.title: Can SAP DRC Be Used with Non-SAP Applications?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m currently exploring SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) for electronic invoicing and legal reporting, and I have a question regarding its flexibility:

Is it possible to implement or integrate SAP DRC with non-SAP applications (e.g., a custom billing or ERP system)? If so, what are the typical approaches for integration, and what technical or licensing considerations should be taken into account?

My goal is to use SAP DRC as the compliance engine while the source data comes from a non-SAP system. I’d appreciate any insights, examples, or recommendations from those who may have done something similar.

Thank you in advance.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

entry.title: How to obtain Packing Time Stamp on Outbound Delivery Status DPC in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi

I am currently using SAP EWM module on SAP S/4 HANA Release 1809, and we observe that in the Monitor, when we click on Outbound Delivery,on the Status tab all statuses are displayed like Time Stamp of picking completed, time stamp of Good Issue , excpet Pack Time DPC , how can I obtain Pack time DPC Stamp in a simplest way. 

The meaning of pack time should be when all the products in the outbound delivery positions are packed in a Shipping HU and packing of all products is completed. 

 

Thank you.

entry.title: xRechnung Germany BT Fields Mapping
entry.content:
Hello together, 

I am looking for customizing to fill the BT fields from the SD order in Document Reporting and Compliance Cloud Edition.

For example BT Field 41 = Seller contact point.

We would like to specify the clerk from the debtor > KNB1-BUSAB

Regards

Marco

entry.title: eDocument High Level Process in SAP
entry.content:
E-Invoice Process in SAP

In recent years there have been legal requirements for many countries to have invoice documents generated electronically and submit to either customer and/or government portal electronically.

In this article I will discuss the high-level generic approach to be followed in SAP to achieve these requirements. There are different country level requirements across the globe and each country level requirement are not covered specifically here. For many countries SAP has provided solutions and be utilized.

 

It is possible that electronic documents are submitted to business partners directly, on the other hand there can be a requirement to submit electronic documents to respective government portals directly. In case of Spain, it is called SII and in case of Italy it is called SDI.

The business process looks as under:

  1. You Create a document in SAP for example Invoice and system creates a eDocument for the same
  2. In the eDocument Cockpit system maps the XML format against these documents and forward it to the services. Depending on the scenario selected, services validate and checks or digitally sign the documents before submission to receiver system.
  3. Receivers’ system send message back, for example acknowledgement or validation code back to the service, in turn forward the message back to SAP application in back end.
  4. The system updates electronica documents and the user can further process them in eDocument Cockpit.
  5. eDocument Cockpit allows you to perform below:
  6. Create, process, and monitor electronic documents for country/region-specific processes (transaction EDOC_COCKPIT).
  7. Import incoming messages and further process them in the system (transaction EDOC_INBOUND_MSG)
  8. Upload incoming messages in form of XML files from your file system to the application server (transaction EDOC_INBOUND_UPLOAD).
  9. Submit several electronic documents bundled in one electronic document (transaction EDOC_SUMMARY).
  10. Automate the processing of several steps to run in the background (transaction EDOC_BACKGROUND).
  11. Set the status of electronic documents to complete (transaction EDOC_COMPLETE).

Source: help.sap.com

For any further question ask mdkhatri1972@gmail.com

entry.title: SAP DRC Statutory Reporting: Setting up correct periodicity of GB_VAT_DCL
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 
I am stuck attempting to set up a GB_VAT_DCL (GB VAT return) whose reporting quarter differs from it's financial year / accounting period. Here's an example: 

Accounting period: Calendar year, i.e. 01/01/2025 - 31/12/2025. 

VAT Reporting periods: Dec 24 - Feb 25 (Q1), Mar 25 - May 25 etc.

I have already managed to create a Fiscal Year Variant reflecting the VAT reporting period accordingly. However, when I wish to enter this new variant in the periodicity section of my GB_VAT_DCL (SM34 ->  SRFVC_RPG_ENT -> GBGRPENTY -> GB_VAT_DCL -> Set periodicity), I get the error message that my reporting period doesn't match my fiscal year variant. 
Has anyone here experienced a similar issue before? If yes, I'd very much appreciate if you could share your solution this problem with me. 
Many thanks, 
Phil

entry.title: HTTP Client is not connceted in S/4HANA 1909
entry.content:
Dear Experts, 

We are using the SAP DRCce product and have followed all the necessary configuration steps for S/4HANA 1909 as outlined in the Help Portal:
"https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/integrate-cloud-edition-with-sap-s-4hana".

However, when submitting an e-invoice via transaction code EDOC_COCKPIT, we encounter the message: "HTTP client is not connected".

We have checked dev_icm, dev_w*, dev_rfc*, and the system log, but there are no related error messages.

Is there an expert who can help resolve this issue?
